design_philosophy.core_loop: Select WWF wrestler, defeat opponents in one-on-one matches through button-mashing combat and grapple sequences to advance toward WWF Championship
design_philosophy.primary_tension: Real-time combat execution against escalating opponent difficulty; managing stamina and momentum during matches
design_philosophy.player_fantasy: Embody a professional wrestler, ascending the ranks to claim the WWF Championship title
design_philosophy.what_makes_it_opinionated: First home console WWF game prioritizes licensed wrestler authenticity and arcade-style beat-em-up directness over simulation; designed as accessible spectacle rather than technical wrestling sim

difficulty.player_agency_in_difficulty: Player selects wrestler with implicit stat variations; no documented difficulty settings

progression.unlock_mechanism: Defeat opponents in sequence to unlock next match; ultimate goal is WWF Championship victory
replayability_hooks: Multiple selectable wrestlers with distinct playstyles

world_building_techniques: Licensed WWF wrestler rosters establish context
Championship progression mimics real wrestling promotion structure

faction_indicators: WWF wrestler selection represents character faction/persona alignment
danger_signals: Championship opponents represent escalating threat level
environmental_storytelling: Wrestling ring setting and referee presence establish competitive sport context

sources_used: tcrf (The Cutting Room Floor - WWF WrestleMania NES article)
